one_time_description: Settlement claim received during the year; also treaty loan forgiveness of $7,797,354 from Federal Government
economic_notes: Yale First Nation has investments in Cascade Lower Canyon Community Forest LP (33%), Smemela:lh Group Management Ltd. (100%), and Stelmexw Enterprises LP (99%). Also holds investments in BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing LP and BC First Nations Gaming Sharing General Partner Ltd.
notes: Audit is qualified due to missing budget comparison information (not a going concern qualification). Large one-time settlement claim of $21,428,571 dominates revenue. Treaty loan forgiveness of $7,797,354 from Federal Government eliminated both an asset (treaty negotiation costs) and long-term debt. Administration & Education expenses jumped from $695,007 to $14,694,212 likely due to settlement-related costs. Three entities accounted for by modified equity method (GBEs). One consolidated subsidiary: Y.F.N. Developments Inc. Salaries total from segmented schedule: $1,006,289. isc_funding includes only ISC line; total_government_transfers includes ISC ($893,968) + First Nation Health Authority ($610,592) + Other government funding ($191,134) + CMHC ($44,266) + Province of BC ($150,112) = $1,889,792 (DFO and Pacific Salmon Commission treated as own-source/other). COVID-19 noted as significant event. No remuneration schedule found in document.
